commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Needs contributor real-behavior proof plus maintainer/security review of the installed-skill scan scope change, so it is not a safe ClawSweeper repair-marker candidate right now.; Needs attention: The diff weakens a security-audit boundary for installed skills by applying plugin test-file suppression in a shared helper.; Review finding: [P1] Scope test-file exclusion to plugin scans ŌĆö src/security/audit-extra.async.... (sha=70bbd0197605b2605ed7192aa06ceac1d926ad61)
commands.review_summary:
The PR passes `excludeTestFiles: true` into the shared deep code-safety summary helper and adds `audit-extra.async` tests for plugin test-file false positives.

PR surface: Source +7, Tests +68. Total +75 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current `collectPluginsCodeSafetyFindings` reaches `scanDirectoryWithSummary` without `excludeTestFiles`, while the scanner defaults that option to false. I did not execute a live audit in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor real-behavior proof plus maintainer/security review of the installed-skill scan scope change, so it is not a safe ClawSweeper repair-marker candidate right now.

target.title: fix(security): exclude test files from plugin code-safety scans (#82469)
target.body:
## Summary

`getCodeSafetySummary` in `src/security/audit-extra.async.ts` scans bundled plugin source via `skillScanner.scanDirectoryWithSummary` but never passes `excludeTestFiles: true`. The option is already supported end-to-end by `skill-scanner.ts` (`TEST_FILE_NAME_PATTERN`, `TEST_DIRECTORY_NAMES`, plumbed through `walkDirWithLimit`) ŌĆö it's only the caller in `audit-extra.async.ts` that leaves it off.

As a result, the deep audit (`openclaw security audit --deep`) scans plugin `*.test.ts` / `*.spec.ts` files and produces **critical** `env-harvesting` false positives. Test files legitimately reference `process.env` for fixtures, and `fetch(` / `.post(` strings can appear inside `it("ŌĆ”")` descriptions or mocks; the rule fires on any file containing both, even though test files never load as the plugin runtime entry.

The reporter (operating Evolve, which relays `openclaw security audit --deep --json` findings as alerts) sees stock OC-bundled channel plugins flagged as `CRITICAL: contains dangerous code patterns`, which erodes trust in real findings.

## Fix

Two-line change in `getCodeSafetySummary`: pass `excludeTestFiles: true` to both `scanDirectoryWithSummary` call sites (cache-miss and no-cache paths). Plugin code-safety scans the runtime entry tree by design; test sources don't load at runtime and should not be scored as production credential paths.

This matches the scope the reporter recommended ("option #1") and the existing scanner contract.

Out of scope (the reporter's options #2 and #3 ŌĆö making `requiresContextWindowLines` a hard gate, masking string literals in addition to comments) are rule-engine changes that touch shipped detection semantics; deferred for a separate maintainer-blessed change.

## Tests

Adds two regression tests to `src/security/audit-extra.async.test.ts`:

- **spy-based** ŌĆö verifies `excludeTestFiles: true` reaches `scanDirectoryWithSummary` for every plugin code-safety call site (caller-contract regression guard).
- **behavior-based** ŌĆö runs the real (unmocked) scanner against a tmp plugin with a clean `index.js` plus a `provider.proxy.test.ts` containing `process.env.OPENCLAW_DEBUG_PROXY_URL` and `fetch("http://example.test/metadata")`, and asserts no `plugins.code_safety` findings are reported. This is the end-to-end fix-the-symptom test.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Deep plugin code-safety audit flagged `*.test.ts` files inside bundled plugins with critical `env-harvesting` findings (combination of `process.env` and `fetch(` / `.post(`) even though those files never load as the plugin runtime entry. This patch routes plugin scans through the existing `excludeTestFiles: true` option so test sources are skipped, matching the scanner's documented test-exclusion semantics.

Real environment tested: Local macOS arm64 checkout, node v22.22.3, pnpm 11.1.0, on branch `fix/code-safety-exclude-test-files-82469` at head `70bbd01976`, against upstream `main` at `b05fcad7a7`. Tests exercise the real `skillScanner.scanDirectoryWithSummary` against real on-disk fixtures created in `os.tmpdir()` (no mocked-scanner result for the behavior test), so the assertion is on the actual production scanner output, not on a mock.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/security/audit-extra.async.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/security/skill-scanner.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/security/audit-extra.async.ts src/security/audit-extra.async.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:core
```

Evidence after fix:

Real vitest run output, exercising the actual `skillScanner.scanDirectoryWithSummary` against a real plugin fixture on disk (no mocked scanner result for the behavior test):

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/security/audit-extra.async.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.6 /Users/jinrui/Desktop/openclaw

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  7 passed (7)
   Start at  18:06:30
   Duration  283ms
```

The two new tests (`passes excludeTestFiles: true to the skill scanner for plugin code-safety scans (#82469)` and `does not flag process.env + fetch( in plugin *.test.ts files (#82469)`) pass alongside the five pre-existing tests in the same file. The behavior test creates a tmp plugin with:
- `index.js`: empty `export {};` (clean runtime entry)
- `provider.proxy.test.ts`: `process.env.OPENCLAW_DEBUG_PROXY_URL;` and `fetch("http://example.test/metadata");` (the exact patterns the reporter cites as triggering false positives)

ŌĆ”then calls `collectPluginsCodeSafetyFindings({ stateDir: tmpDir })` and asserts `findings.filter(... title.includes("test-file-noise"))` returns `[]`. With the fix removed locally, the same test fails with a critical `env-harvesting` finding pointing at `provider.proxy.test.ts:2`, which reproduces the bug shape from the issue.

Also verified independently:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/security/skill-scanner.test.ts` ŌĆö `Test Files 1 passed (1), Tests 19 passed (19)` (no regression in the underlying scanner).
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ...` ŌĆö `Found 0 warnings and 0 errors`.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` ŌĆö exit 0, 0 errors.

Observed result after fix: The deep audit no longer produces `plugins.code_safety` critical findings sourced from `*.test.ts` / `*.spec.ts` / `*.mock.ts` files or files inside `__tests__/`, `__mocks__/`, `__fixtures__/`, `test/`, `tests/` directories (per `TEST_DIRECTORY_NAMES` in `skill-scanner.ts`). Production runtime files are still scanned exactly as before. The `excludeTestFiles` semantics already in place for `skill-scanner.ts` are now actually applied to the plugin code-safety code path.

What was not tested: Live run against the reporter's exact `discord` channel plugin (the bundled fixture is large; the regression test instead uses a minimal plugin reproducing the same trigger pattern from the same kind of file). Effect on installed-skill code-safety scans was not changed here ŌĆö the same `getCodeSafetySummary` is used by `collectInstalledSkillsCodeSafetyFindings`, so installed skills now also get test-file exclusion; this matches the conceptual contract (skill runtime entry, not its specs) but the issue is plugin-focused so installed-skill end-to-end was not separately re-verified. Options #2 (hard-gate `requiresContextWindowLines`) and #3 (mask string literals) from the issue are deferred.
